# Team News: Brazil vs. Norway injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Seye Omidiora| 04 Jul 2026 21:00, Last updated: 04 Jul 2026 21:00

© Iconsport / Leo Barrilari/SPP

Aiming to finally get the better of Norway, Brazil face the Vikings in Sunday's World Cup 2026 round of 16 match at MetLife Stadium.

Carlo Ancelotti's men needed a second-half turnaround to beat Japan in the round of 32, while the European outfit have thrived on their return to the Mundial after 28 years, beating Ivory Coast 2-1 in the previous round.

Here Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## BRAZIL vs. NORWAY

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/world-cup/brazil-vs-norway_game_249413.html

## BRAZIL

Out: Raphinha(hamstring), Lucas Paqueta(muscle)

Doubtful: Casemiro(muscle)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Alisson; Danilo, Marquinhos, Gabriel, Douglas Santos; Guimaraes, Casemiro, Danilo Santos; Rayan, Cunha, Vini Jr.

## NORWAY

Out: None

Doubtful: Julian Ryerson(thigh)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Nyland;

🇧🇷 Brazil v Norway: likely line-ups, absentees and chances

Brazil and Norway face off this Sunday (5) at 5 p.m. at New Jersey Stadium for a place in the FIFA World Cup quarterfinals.

The winner will face Mexico or England. The loser goes home. A draw sends the match to extra time and, if necessary, penalties.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Who will win, Brazil or Norway? And who should replace Lucas Paquetá? Leave your take in the comments box!

## Brazil lineup 🇧🇷

Raphinha has recovered and is expected to start on the bench. That leaves Lucas Paquetá as the only absentee, with a thigh injury.

Carlo Ancelotti could start Danilo Santos and keep the 4-3-3 formation.

Another option, and the more likely one, is for Gabriel Martinelli to get the spot. In that case, Matheus Cunha would drop back into midfield.

Endrick and Neymar are other options to replace Paquetá, but they are outsiders.

# Brazil's predicted lineup to face Norway in the World Cup 2026 Round of 16

## Brazil continues to build momentum at the FIFA World Cup 2026 and now faces one of its toughest tests of the tournament.

Brazil continues to build momentum at the FIFA World Cup 2026 and now faces one of its toughest tests of the tournament.

After eliminating a resilient Japan side, Carlo Ancelotti's team will battle Erling Haaland's Norway for a place in the quarterfinals.

Brazil advanced after a hard-fought victory over Japan, while Norway earned its spot by defeating Ivory Coast.

#### Follow all the FIFA World Cup coverage on beINSPORTS

## Ancelotti on stopping Haaland

One of the biggest storylines heading into the match is Brazil's attempt to contain Erling Haaland.

However, Ancelotti dismissed the idea of creating a special tactical plan focused solely on the Norwegian striker.

"There is no anti-Haaland plan. I'm not going to explain to Gabriel, who has faced him many ti

news_items.title: Brazil projected 11 vs. Norway with Raphinha and Paqueta ruled out - Yahoo Sports
news_items.author: Kristie Ackert, USA TODAY Sun, July 5, 2026 at 10:04 AM UTC · 1 min read
news_items.text:
Brazil projected 11 vs. Norway with Raphinha and Paqueta ruled out - Yahoo Sports

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

TRENDING

France gets past Paraguay

Morocco reaches quarters

MLB All-Star rosters

Hot dog! Chestnut wins again

Wimbledon: Serena withdraws

Story by

Brazil needed a stoppage time winner to get past Japan in the round of 32, and it heads into a knockout match against Norway at MetLife Stadium on Sunday, June 5 carrying a lot of injury concerns. That leaves coach Carlo Ancelotti with more to sort out than any of the other favorites remaining in the field.

## Projected Brazil lineup vs. Norway

Advertisement

Advertisement

Defenders: Danillo, Marquinhos, Gabriel, Douglas Santos

Midfielders: Bruno Guimaraes, Casemiro

Attacking midfielders: Rayan, Matheus Cunha, Vinicius Junior

## Who is out for Brazil?

Raphinha is not yet fit enough to return and Lucas Paqueta has been ruled out with a hamstring injury. Meanwhile, Wesley's earlier injury already pushed the veteran Danilo to right back.

Casemiro limped out of the Japan game, but is still expected to play on Sunday.

More: Brazil vs Norway World Cup round of 16 expert prediction, odds,

# Team News: Brazil vs. Norway injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Seye Omidiora| 04 Jul 2026 21:00, Last updated: 04 Jul 2026 21:00

© Iconsport / Leo Barrilari/SPP

Aiming to finally get the better of Norway, Brazil face the Vikings in Sunday's World Cup 2026 round of 16 match at MetLife Stadium.

Carlo Ancelotti's men needed a second-half turnaround to beat Japan in the round of 32, while the European outfit have thrived on their return to the Mundial after 28 years, beating Ivory Coast 2-1 in the previous round.

Here Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## BRAZIL vs. NORWAY

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/world-cup/brazil-vs-norway_game_249413.html

## BRAZIL

Out: Raphinha(hamstring), Lucas Paqueta(muscle)

Doubtful: Casemiro(muscle)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Alisson; Danilo, Marquinhos, Gabriel, Douglas Santos; Guimaraes, Casemiro, Danilo Santos; Rayan, Cunha, Vini Jr.

## NORWAY

Out: None

Doubtful: Julian Ryerson(thigh)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Nyland; "}

🇧🇷 Brazil v Norway: likely line-ups, absentees and chances

Brazil and Norway face off this Sunday (5) at 5 p.m. at New Jersey Stadium for a place in the FIFA World Cup quarterfinals.

The winner will face Mexico or England. The loser goes home. A draw sends the match to extra time and, if necessary, penalties.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Who will win, Brazil or Norway? And who should replace Lucas Paquetá? Leave your take in the comments box!

## Brazil lineup 🇧🇷

Raphinha has recovered and is expected to start on the bench. That leaves Lucas Paquetá as the only absentee, with a thigh injury.

Carlo Ancelotti could start Danilo Santos and keep the 4-3-3 formation.

Another option, and the more likely one, is for Gabriel Martinelli to get the spot. In that case, Matheus Cunha would drop back into midfield.

Endrick and Neymar are other options to replace Paquetá, but they are outsiders.

# Brazil's predicted lineup to face Norway in the World Cup 2026 Round of 16

## Brazil continues to build momentum at the FIFA World Cup 2026 and now faces one of its toughest tests of the tournament.

Brazil continues to build momentum at the FIFA World Cup 2026 and now faces one of its toughest tests of the tournament.

After eliminating a resilient Japan side, Carlo Ancelotti's team will battle Erling Haaland's Norway for a place in the quarterfinals.

Brazil advanced after a hard-fought victory over Japan, while Norway earned its spot by defeating Ivory Coast.

#### Follow all the FIFA World Cup coverage on beINSPORTS

## Ancelotti on stopping Haaland

One of the biggest storylines heading into the match is Brazil's attempt to contain Erling Haaland.

However, Ancelotti dismissed the idea of creating a special tactical plan focused solely on the Norwegian striker.

"There is no anti-Haaland plan. I'm not going to explain to Gabriel, who has faced him many ti"}
{"title": "Brazil projected 11 vs. Norway with Raphinha and Paqueta ruled out - Yahoo Sports", "url": "https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/brazil-projected-11-vs-norway-100407605.html", "publishedDate": null, "author": "Kristie Ackert, USA TODAY Sun, July 5, 2026 at 10:04 AM UTC · 1 min read", "text": "Brazil projected 11 vs. Norway with Raphinha and Paqueta ruled out - Yahoo Sports

Story by

Brazil needed a stoppage time winner to get past Japan in the round of 32, and it heads into a knockout match against Norway at MetLife Stadium on Sunday, June 5 carrying a lot of injury concerns. That leaves coach Carlo Ancelotti with more to sort out than any of the other favorites remaining in the field.

## Projected Brazil lineup vs. Norway

Advertisement

Advertisement

Defenders: Danillo, Marquinhos, Gabriel, Douglas Santos

Midfielders: Bruno Guimaraes, Casemiro

Attacking midfielders: Rayan, Matheus Cunha, Vinicius Junior

## Who is out for Brazil?

Raphinha is not yet fit enough to return and Lucas Paqueta has been ruled out with a hamstring injury. Meanwhile, Wesley's earlier injury already pushed the veteran Danilo to right back.

Casemiro limped out of the Japan game, but is still expected to play on Sunday.
